basics.name: Muhammad Umair Khan
basics.label: QA Automation Architect
basics.headline: I architect quality systems that give engineering teams speed, confidence, and executive-level clarity.
basics.summary: QA Automation Architect based in Karachi, Pakistan. I lead automation strategy across web, mobile, desktop, and API ecosystems, build durable frameworks from the ground up, and help organizations turn quality into a delivery advantage instead of a release bottleneck.
basics.availability: Currently leading quality architecture at Contour Software since February 2026.
basics.leadershipNote: Trusted by product and engineering leaders to bring structure, visibility, and dependable release confidence to complex delivery environments.

intro: I specialize in turning fragmented QA efforts into durable quality platforms that scale with product complexity. My work sits at the intersection of architecture, delivery strategy, test engineering, and organizational enablement.

Over the last several years, I have led automation initiatives across telecom, healthcare, SaaS, and enterprise tooling, building frameworks from scratch, coaching teams, and creating the systems leaders rely on when release confidence matters.
strengths: Framework architecture for web, mobile, desktop, and API testing
Quality strategy for distributed engineering teams and fast release cycles
CI/CD integration, reporting, and release confidence automation
Mentoring QA engineers and aligning stakeholders around measurable quality outcomes

leadershipPillars.title: Architecture
leadershipPillars.description: Designing maintainable automation ecosystems that support scale instead of creating future drag.

leadershipPillars.title: Leadership
leadershipPillars.description: Guiding QA engineers, influencing stakeholders, and building stronger engineering habits around quality.

leadershipPillars.title: Impact
leadershipPillars.description: Connecting test strategy to faster releases, clearer visibility, and higher confidence in production readiness.

metrics.label: Years building and scaling automation systems
Core test surfaces across modern product stacks
Industries supported with architecture-level QA leadership
Leadership across distributed automation and QA functions

work.summary: Leading quality architecture initiatives, defining automation strategy, and establishing scalable test systems that strengthen release confidence across product teams.
highlights: Defined architecture patterns for scalable automation across multiple products
Aligned quality engineering practices with business and release objectives
Established a stronger foundation for maintainable test coverage and faster delivery
work.impact: Owning the quality architecture direction and setting the standard for how automation supports product delivery at scale.

work.summary: Built a hybrid automation framework from scratch for STC Play and STC TV across web, mobile, and API layers, while leading multiple automation streams and coordinating mobile QA delivery.
highlights: Built a hybrid automation framework from the ground up
Integrated web, mobile, and API testing into one delivery model
Led web, mobile, and backend automation teams
Managed a manual QA team focused on mobile quality
Set up Jenkins pipelines for daily execution and reporting
work.impact: Created a unified quality operation for a large-scale digital platform by combining architecture, leadership, and delivery discipline.

work.summary: Owned end-to-end automation strategy for mobile healthcare products using Appium, Selenium, Java, and TestNG, improving release confidence for a product with high reliability requirements.
highlights: Spearheaded end-to-end automation for mobile applications
Expanded coverage using Appium, Selenium, Java, and TestNG
Improved stability and confidence for critical healthcare workflows
work.impact: Brought stronger rigor and predictability to a healthcare product environment where quality and trust were non-negotiable.

work.summary: Built desktop automation capabilities with Appium, C#, and WinAppDriver, and supported test development with Robot Framework for broader product validation.
highlights: Built desktop automation frameworks for Windows applications
Used Appium, C#, and Microsoft WinAppDriver
Created additional coverage with Robot Framework and Python
work.impact: Expanded the automation footprint into desktop validation and helped reduce dependence on repetitive manual verification.

work.summary: Automated regression testing for a web-based platform using Selenium and Python, integrating CI/CD to reduce effort and improve confidence in release patches.
highlights: Automated regression with Selenium WebDriver and Python
Integrated automation into CI/CD delivery flows
Reduced manual effort while improving release confidence
work.impact: Helped establish the early foundation for repeatable, automation-driven release practices.

projects.title: Unified QA Platform
projects.description: Brought web, mobile, and API coverage into a single maintainable automation architecture for large-scale digital products with clearer ownership, reporting, and execution discipline.
projects.outcome: Turned fragmented testing efforts into a more coherent quality platform that leaders could rely on for release readiness.

projects.title: Desktop Automation Framework
projects.description: Built framework foundations for Windows-based product validation to improve repeatability, reduce manual effort, and expand the automation surface beyond web-only testing.
projects.outcome: Added durable desktop validation capability where automation maturity had previously been limited.

projects.title: Release Confidence Pipelines
projects.description: Designed automated execution pipelines, quality gates, and reporting flows that made release status more visible and gave teams a stronger operational rhythm.
projects.outcome: Improved the connection between test execution, decision-making, and delivery confidence.

education.institution: National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences
